payload.title: Capital Program Committee Meeting
payload.description: Capital Program Committee Meeting book including meeting minutes, work plan, integrated project updates, IEC project reviews, safety report, capital program status, and procurements for January 2023

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: MTA Construction & Development Company, responsible for capital program delivery
MTA Independent Engineering Consultant
MTA Independent Engineering Consultant

Provide Metro-North Railroad New Haven Line customers with service into and out of Penn Station via Amtrak's Hell Gate Line. Includes four new ADA-accessible stations in the East Bronx, bridge rehabilitations, almost 20 miles of new and rehabilitated track work, new and reconfigured interlockings, modernization of signal, power and communication infrastructure, and expansion of Metro-North's New Rochelle Yard.

Addresses major congestion and passenger improvements at the 33 St Concourse in Penn Station. Widens corridor to 57 feet and raises ceiling to 18 feet. Includes passenger amenities, station and facilities upgrades, LIRR SOGR and ADA work, NYCT SOGR and ADA work, and MTAPD priority work.
payload.location: Penn Station, 33 St Concourse, New York, NY

location_normalized.raw_text: Penn Station, 33 St Concourse, New York, NY

payload.description: Provides LIRR passengers with direct service to Grand Central Terminal. Involved building an 11.5-mile underground tunnel with a new station and concourse below the existing GCT. Revenue Service Date of January 25, 2023.

Added a 10-mile-long third track on the LIRR Main Line reducing train congestion and enabling bi-directional service during peak hours. Included elimination of eight street level grade crossings, improvements to rail bridges, reconstruction of five stations, and new parking garages.

Part of the Northeast Corridor Congestion Relief Project to reconstruct Harold Interlocking to create two grade-separated routes (Eastbound Reroute and Westbound Bypass) for Amtrak and Metro-North trains traveling between Penn Station and the Hell Gate and New Haven Lines.
Design-build services for a new Jamaica Bus Depot, the MTA's first zero-emissions bus facility. Contract awarded to Skanska USA Civil Northeast Inc. for $483,775,300.
Wayside communication-based train control system on the New York City Transit G Line from Court Square station to Church Avenue station. Contract awarded to Crosstown Partners for $405,779,259.
Rehabilitation and flood resiliency work along New York City Transit's Rockaway Line in Queens. Contract awarded to Schiavone Construction Co. LLC for $392,685,186.
Phase 1 of the replacement of Metro-North Railroad's Park Avenue Viaduct in Manhattan. Contract awarded to Halmar International for $382,297,027.

tool_args.label: Coney Island Yard Complex Long Term Flood Mitigation

payload.project_name: Coney Island Yard Complex Long Term Flood Mitigation
payload.description: Flood mitigation at the Coney Island Yard Complex in Brooklyn. Contract C-34836 with Tully Construction Co. Inc. Includes construction of a flood wall along the perimeter of the Coney Island Yard Complex extending approximately 30 feet below grade and approximately 12-15 feet above grade.
Capital Program Committee Meeting at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor Board Room, New York, NY, 8:30 AM - 12:00 PM ET
Capital Program Committee Meeting, New York, New York, 12:45 P.M. Meeting called to order at 2:05 P.M. and adjourned at 2:46 PM.
LIRR direct shuttle service from Jamaica to Grand Central Madison commenced
New York State Governor Kathy Hochul announced the groundbreaking for the Metro-North Penn Station Access project

tool_args.label: MNPSA Delays Due to Amtrak Access

payload.claim_text: The project has experienced significant construction delays (between 6 and 9 months) due to limited access onto the Amtrak ROW and lack of Amtrak Force Account support
payload.description: Metro-North Penn Station Access project delays due to Amtrak access constraints
payload.subject: Metro-North Penn Station Access

payload.claim_text: If Amtrak is able to provide the level of Force Account support that was committed to in the 2021 Cost Sharing Agreement, the project may be able to recover and meet the original Substantial Completion date of March 2027

tool_args.label: Coney Island Yard Signal Cable Damage

payload.missing_information: As-built drawings did not depict signal cables at the location where they were severed
payload.description: On February 17, 2022, while installing 40-foot steel sheet piles along Shore Road near Gate 4 of the Coney Island Yard Complex, the contractor inadvertently severed a bundle of underground signal cables. There were no indications or signal monuments identifying the existence of signal cables in the vicinity nor were signal cables depicted in the as-built drawings provided to the contractor.
Metro-North Penn Station Access had a groundbreaking announced by NYS Governor Kathy Hochul in December 2022
